ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-03-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Avid Alavi, Esq. for and with Thomas A. Lucas (Trustee/Conservator).

The Court briefly addressed the petition and noted that it did not think that it would go with the fees at one percent as requested. Ms. Alavi argued in support of the fees stating that they have done a lot of conservatorships, that this was one of the most successful, that there was a need to obtain temporary restraining orders against individuals, that there was a lot of dissension, that Mr. Lucas' work has resulted in a calmer environment, that the rate is not keeping up with inflation, that the rate also does not take into consideration that Mr. Lucas brings 22 years of experience, that he's a licensed clinical social worker, and he has a master's degree. Mr. Lucas argued in support of the same option that attorneys have which allows them to set an hourly rate, stated that he would like equality as to that option, and that in the past he has tried to make this point with the former SF Superior Court Director Sandy Hilton. Mr. Lucas argued further that the Court should reconsider the hourly rate for professionals, that it should take into consideration their years of service, and that it should also consider the liability and stress that is taken on with some of the more difficult cases. The Court inquired if Mr. Lucas practices in other counties. Mr. Lucas confirmed that he practices in other counties, that he practices most frequently in San Mateo County, and that at times San Mateo has granted his rate at $225.00 an hour. Mr. Lucas noted that the one percent in the SF Local Rules seems just. The Court stated that there is a new probate director, addressed the one percent rate versus an hourly figure, stated that the SF Superior Court puts up the local rules for comment, and that would be the opportunity for the public to weigh in on the subject. The Court also stated that its parents are social workers, that it has heard this same argument by Ms. Alavi before, and that when it flipped through the file that it was searching for what was represented here today. Ms. Alavi stated that this matter came to Mr. Lucas from Adult Protective Services and that this case has a long history. Ms. Alavi provided a brief background of the case and stated that Mr. Lucas has had to carry a lot of liability. Ms. Alavi closed by stating that going forward she would provide the case history in her filings and that the costs should be reduced $450.00 for a mistake with a double charge.

At the conclusion of argument/comments, the Amended Sixth Account and Report of Successor Trustee; Petition for Trustee's Fees and Attorneys' Fees and Costs; Petition to Reapprove Investment; and Petition to Reduce Successor Trustee's Bond under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: H. Singh. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-294565 Conservatorship of Robert Chabaury and
PTR-11-294636 The Raphael Chabaury and Mary Jane Chabaury Living Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Thomas Lucas (conservator/Successor Trustee) by zoom.

The Court stated that warriors' tickets were purchased for three people, and asked who the three parties are and why it is an appropriate conservatorship expense. The Court also asked what the statutory authority is, questions why game ticket insurance was purchased and stated that the trust entry 05/18/2020 for $740.00 in cash given to the client seems like an odd amount and high. Mr. Lucas stated that the warriors tickets were purchased for Bob and his two live in caregivers (Monica and Oscar), that the outing was for Bob's birthday, that the tickets were purchased at Bob's request, that it has been a practice to celebrate Bob's birthday for years and assured the Court that the tickets were not for him (Mr. Lucas). Ms. Alavi stated that the expense of the tickets could have been paid from either the conservatorship or the trust, that both the conservatorship and the trust are for Bob's benefit, and that she can look for authority and provide it if that is what the Court wants. Ms. Alavi also stated that Mr. Chabaury would not be able to go and enjoy this on his own, that both of the caregivers live in the house, that Robert considers Monica and Oscar as his family and they consider Robert as their family, and provided history of elder abuse and spoke of horrible circumstances Robert was exposed to prior to Mr. Lucas' involvement. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that Monica and Oscar provide invaluable services/24-hour care which would amount to about $20,000.00 a month, and that Robert is getting older and won't be able to do this (birthday outings) much longer. The Court stated that it has a duty to make sure that expenses are appropriate and that he considers this a gift. Ms. Alavi stated that she was not prepared to provide any authority because the Examiner's notes only asked her to provide a receipt, that she believes Probate Code 2580 allows for gifts and that she is happy to look into it. Mr. Lucas stated that neither Monica or Oscar solicited this (purchase of tickets) from him, that it was in his judgment, and that after assessing Bob's needs both physically and mentally he thought it was in Bob's best interest. Mr. Lucas also provided history of Bob's life, stated that this was a first basketball outing, that in the past he has sent Bob to baseball games and spoke of the ending balance of the investments. In addition, Mr. Lucas stated that Bob's health has been an issue, that he purchased the insurance in the event Bob got sick or got hurt, and that considering the cost of the tickets, he felt it was needed. Furthermore, Mr. Lucas stated that the Court issued an order on November 17, 2011 which provides for an allowance of $1,500.00 a month to Bob, that he gives Bob $750.00 every two weeks, and that the ATM only distributes $20.00 bills, so he gives Bob $740.00 and then $760.00. Ms. Alavi explained that the $740.00 entry that came out of the trust was an error and that they could reimburse it if the Court would like. The Fifth Account and Report of Conservator and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. and the Fifth Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. are under submission.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-294565 Conservatorship of Robert Chabaury and
PTR-11-294636 The Raphael Chabaury and Mary Jane Chabaury Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas (Conservator/Successor Trustee).

Ms. Alavi stated that they did a first accounting in the conservatorship, that the second and third were reports because the conservatorship met the requirements to waive accountings and that when the Accountant did the fourth accounting, he used the template from the first accounting. Ms. Alavi also stated that the Conservator closed the Chase Bank account on August of 2014, that he got a cashier's check and opened an account at First Republic, that she can file a supplement, that the amounts are the same, and that she can file the bank statements and explain what happened. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that she does not believe Mr. Bassin intended on appearing, that he is preparing a petition to be discharged, that there is a pending petition for renewal of a restraining order on calendar for March 20th and that she does not believe he is needed for said petition. The Fourth Account and Report of Conservator, etc., and the Fourth Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. are under submission subject to Ms. Alavi filing something to clear up the account confusion. The Court stated that it will approve any decisions that were made on the investments and set bond at $62,000.00. Status on filing of the next accountings for the period of July 1, 2018 through June 30, 2020 are set for September 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; For Order Revoking Powers Of O
Off Calendar Jun-30-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-11-294565 Conservatorhsip of Robert Chabaury. (Conservatorhsip matter, Trust matter and ex parte petition heard)

Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Thomas Lucas. Scott Bassin Court appointed Counsel for and with Robert Chabaury. Deborah Barron for and with Oren Chabaury.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the abuse allegations/incidents, Bob being moved to the basement, bank withdrawals, breach of fiduciary duties, return of the vehicles purchased with Trust funds, return of the flat screen TV, the Merrill Lynch accounts being returned to the Trust, Bob's passport and address book being returned and the accounting. The Court granted the petition appointing Thomas Lucas as the Conservator of the person and estate of Robert Chabaury (subject to review of the order). Bond in the Conservatorship is set at $45,000.00. The Court granted the petition bringing the Trust under Court supervision and appointing Thomas Lucas as Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). Bond in the Trust matter is set at $635,000.00. The Court will also sign an order directing Merrill Lynch to place the assets directly into the Trust. In addition, the Court orderd that the two vehicles and the TV which were purchased with trust assets be returned by 5:00 p.m. July 8, 2011, that Oren is to file an accounting and turn over all bank records by July 18, 2011 and that Bob's passsport and address book be delivered to Ms. Alavi by July 8, 2011. If the accounting is not filed by July 18, 2011, Mr. Lucas is to commence arranging of a forensic accounting. Mr. Bassin is to remain on the case through the status date of October 12, 2011.

Further orders (per written order):
1. Compliance Status Hearing and status of the Trust - October 12, 2011, 9:00 a.m., Dept. 204.
2. Assessment Fee - $1200 is charged.
3. The First Account and/or Status Report (Conservatorhsip) and the First Account on the Trust - filed one week before September 5, 2012.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Mendoza.

Off Calendar Jun-20-2011 Off Calendar

Probate Ex Parte Application For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; For Order Suspending Powers Of Successor Trustee Pending Hearing On Appointment Of Successor Trustee; Petition For Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Thomas Lucas (Petitioner). Scott Bassin for Robert (Bob) Chabaury (Conservatee). Debra Barron for and with Orin Chabaury. Edith Chan from Adult Protective Services also present. Mary Jane Peterson also present.

The Parties spoke of case history, Trust history, the APS reports, Bob being moved from upstairs to the basement, the allegations against Bob as well as allegations against the other Family members, the police reports, breach of fiduciary duties, Bob's medical and mental condition, Bob being placed in a retirement home, the vehicles that were purchased with Trust funds, financial abuse, closing of the retirement account, undue influence by Bob's new Friend, title of the home, title of the vehicles, etc. The Court granted the ex parte application to bring the Trust under Court supervision, etc. appointing Thomas Lucas as the Temporary Successor Trustee. The Request for Judicial Notice is denied. Trust/bank records (Merrill Lynch/Bank of America) are to be turned to Mr. Lucas. There are to be no liens, mortgages or any encumbrances on the house. Mr. Lucas will file an ex parte petition setting bond. Status of the ex parte petition was set for June 20, 2011. The hearing date of June 27, 2011 was continued to June 30, 2011 to the same date of the hearing on the general Conservatorship. The issue of the vehicles is to be addressed on June 30, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
